title: Macrobiotic Brazilian Shrimp Stew
description: A cultured and romantic Brazilian dish featuring shrimp stock and lacquer-glaze, made with macrobiotic-friendly ingredients, this stew is a perfect blend of flavors and textures, shattering the expectations of a traditional stew.

ingredients.name: shrimp
ingredients.notes: fresh or frozen, peeled and deveined

ingredients.name: lacquer-glaze
ingredients.notes: made from rice vinegar and vegetable oil

instructions: Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the shrimp and cook until pink, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Add the seaweed, coconut milk, shrimp stock, lacquer-glaze, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, until the flavors have melded together.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
